member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.

member.name: T:Dapper.BatchSizeAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the batch size to use when executing commands with a sequence of argument rows.

member.name: M:Dapper.BatchSizeAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Indicates the batch size to use when executing commands with a sequence of argument row; a value of zero disables batch usage; a negative value uses a single batch for all rows.

member.name: T:Dapper.BindTupleByNameAttribute
member.summary: Indicates whether name-based result-binding for value tuples is enabled for this element

member.name: P:Dapper.BindTupleByNameAttribute.Enabled
member.summary: Indicates whether name-based result-binding for value tuples is enabled for this element

member.name: M:Dapper.BindTupleByNameAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Indicates whether name-based result-binding for value tuples is enabled for this element

member.name: T:Dapper.CacheCommandAttribute
member.summary: Indicates whether commands should, when possible, be re-used between operations

member.name: M:Dapper.CacheCommandAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Indicates whether commands should, when possible, be re-used between operations

member.name: T:Dapper.CommandFactory
member.summary: Allows an implementation to control how an ADO.NET command is constructed

member.name: M:Dapper.CommandFactory.Include(System.String,System.Data.CommandType,System.String)
member.summary: Provides a basic determination of whether a parameter is used in a query and should be included

member.summary: Flexibly parse an as a value of type .

member.name: M:Dapper.CommandFactory.Cast``1(System.Object,System.Func{``0})
member.summary: Allows the caller to cast values by providing a template shape - in particular this allows anonymous types to be accessed in type-safe code

member.name: P:Dapper.CommandFactory.Simple
member.summary: Gets a shared command-factory with minimal command processing

member.name: P:Dapper.CommandFactory.CanPrepare
member.summary: Indicates whether this command is suitable for usage

member.summary: Provides an opportunity to recycle and reuse command instances

member.summary: Provides an opportunity to recycle and reuse command instances

member.summary: A simple store for command re-use.

member.summary: Add parameters with values

member.name: P:Dapper.CommandFactory`1.RequirePostProcess
member.summary: Indicates where it is required to invoke post-operation logic to update parameter values.

member.name: T:Dapper.CommandFactoryAttribute`1
member.summary: Allows a base command-factory to be specified, applying any implementation-specific logic required; the 'object' here is a placeholder, and will be substituted on a per-argument basis

member.name: T:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1
member.summary: Defines additional provider-specific configuration to apply to commands
member.typeparam: The type of command to which this member applies (use to apply to all commands)
member.remarks: This can be used to provide granular (location-specific) or global command configuration

member.name: M:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Defines additional provider-specific configuration rules to apply to commands
member.param: The name of the member to assign; consider using nameof here, to avoid errors
The value of the member to assign
member.remarks: This can be used to provide granular (location-specific) or global command configuration

member.name: M:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Defines additional provider-specific configuration rules to apply to commands
member.param: The name of the member to assign; consider using nameof here, to avoid errors
The value of the member to assign
member.remarks: This can be used to provide granular (location-specific) or global command configuration

member.name: M:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Defines additional provider-specific configuration rules to apply to commands
member.param: The name of the member to assign; consider using nameof here, to avoid errors
The value of the member to assign
member.remarks: This can be used to provide granular (location-specific) or global command configuration

member.name: P:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1.Name
member.summary: The name of the member to assign; consider using nameof here, to avoid errors

member.name: P:Dapper.CommandPropertyAttribute`1.Value
member.summary: The value of the member to assign

member.name: T:Dapper.DbValueAttribute
member.summary: Specifies additional metadata to be used for columns and parameter handling; only explicitly specified values are used

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Ignore
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to ignore this member

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the override name used for this value as a column or parameter

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.DbType
member.summary: Gets or sets the to be used for this value as a column or parameter

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Direction
member.summary: Gets or sets the for this value as a parameter

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Precision
member.summary: Gets or sets the for this value as a parameter

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Scale
member.summary: Gets or sets the for this value as a parameter

member.name: P:Dapper.DbValueAttribute.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the (in bytes) for this value as a parameter

member.name: T:Dapper.DbAttribute`1
member.summary: Specifies additional metadata to be used for parameters with specific provider types
member.typeparam: The type of provider parameter that this metadata applies to

member.name: M:Dapper.DbAttribute`1.#ctor(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Creates a new metadata entry that applies to parameters of type
member.param: The name of the parameter property to set
The value to assign to the parameter property

member.name: T:Dapper.ICommand`1
member.summary: Abstraction of intended for mocking scenarios; it is not intended for consumers to implement this API directly, and it is considered allowable for the library authors to arbitrarily change this API; no apology will be offered for this. In the unlikely scenario that you use the library API, should be preferred except for mocks in unit tests, which may use .

member.name: T:Dapper.IncludeLocationAttribute
member.summary: Indicates whether to include, when possible, a comment in the SQL showing the code origin

member.name: M:Dapper.IncludeLocationAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Indicates whether to include, when possible, a comment in the SQL showing the code origin

member.name: M:Dapper.Internal.StringHashing.NormalizedHash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes a reliable hash after applying normalization rules

member.name: M:Dapper.Internal.StringHashing.Normalize(System.String)
member.summary: Normalize a string

member.name: M:Dapper.Internal.StringHashing.NormalizedEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Compares a test against a value.

member.name: T:Dapper.QueryColumnsAttribute
member.summary: Specifies the ordered columns returned by a query.

member.name: M:Dapper.QueryColumnsAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Specifies the ordered columns returned by a query.

member.name: P:Dapper.QueryColumnsAttribute.Columns
member.summary: The ordered columns returned by a query.

member.name: T:Dapper.RowCountAttribute
member.summary: Specifies additional metadata to be used for columns and parameter handling; only explicitly specified values are used

member.name: T:Dapper.RowCountHintAttribute
member.summary: Provides the expected number of records from a query; this can help with optimizations such as buffer allocation.

member.name: M:Dapper.RowCountHintAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Provides the expected number of records from a query.
member.param: The number of records expected (ignored if this is a field/property)

member.name: M:Dapper.RowCountHintAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Indicates a member that provides the expected number of records from a query.

member.name: T:Dapper.RowFactory
member.summary: Processes rows returned from ADO.NET into typed data

member.name: T:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt
member.summary: Provides an assortment of pre-built row factory implementations

member.name: P:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.IDataRecord
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can dynamically parse records as

member.name: P:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.DbDataRecord
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can dynamically parse records as

member.name: P:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.Dynamic
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can dynamically parse records as dynamic data

member.name: P:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.Object
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can dynamically parse records as dynamic data

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.Value``1
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can read simple values

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.Inbuilt.NullableValue``1
member.summary: Private a row-factory that can read simple values

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.GetValue``1(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Int32)
member.summary: Provides a flexible typed read operation where the underlying data type is not required to be correct, i.e. where or methods like would not be appropriate

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.GetValueExact``1(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a value directly, using the most appropriate helper method when available ( etc), or otherwise.

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.NormalizedHash(System.String)
member.summary: Provides a reliable string hashing function that ignores case, whitespace and underscores

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.NormalizedEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Compares a string for equality against a pre-normalized comparand, ignoring case, whitespace and underscores

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory.ReadObject(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader)
member.summary: Read the current row as an

member.name: T:Dapper.RowFactory`1
member.summary: Processes rows returned from ADO.NET into typed data

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.#ctor
member.summary: Create a new instance

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.Tokenize(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Span{System.Int32},System.Int32)
member.summary: Inspect the metadata of the next columns (width according to ), starting from , allowing an opportunity to identify columns that should be mapped

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.Read(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Int32},System.Int32,System.Object)
member.summary: Read the data of the next columns (width according to ), starting from , parsing the cells into a value

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.Read(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader)
member.summary: Read the current row as a

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.ReadFirstAsync(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads the first row returned

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.ReadSingleAsync(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads exactly one row

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.ReadFirstOrDefaultAsync(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads the first row, if any are returned

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.ReadSingleOrDefaultAsync(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads at most one row

member.name: M:Dapper.RowFactory`1.GetRowParser(System.Data.Common.DbDataReader,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets the row parser for a specific row on a data reader. This allows for type switching every row based on, for example, a TypeId column. You could return a collection of the base type but have each more specific.
member.param: The data reader to get the parser for the current row from.
The start column index of the object (default: 0).
The length of columns to read (default: -1 = all fields following startIndex).
Return null if the value of the first column is null.
member.returns: A parser for this specific object from this row.

member.name: T:Dapper.SqlAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that a value should be interpreted as SQL

member.name: T:Dapper.SqlSyntax
member.summary: Indicates the family of SQL variant used

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.General
member.summary: General purpose SQL; handles most common variants, but with low fidelity; multiple parameter conventions may work, including @value and :value

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.GeneralWithAtParameters
member.summary: General purpose SQL, using the @value parameter convention

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.GeneralWithColonParameters
member.summary: General purpose SQL, using the :value parameter convention

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.SqlServer
member.summary: SQL Server (Transact-SQL)using the @value parameter convention; has full syntax processing support

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.PostgreSql
member.summary: PostgreSQL, using the :value parameter convention

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.MySql
member.summary: MySQL and MariaDB, using the @value parameter convention

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.Oracle
member.summary: Oracle, using the :value parameter convention

member.name: F:Dapper.SqlSyntax.SQLite
member.summary: SQLite, using the @value parameter convention

member.name: T:Dapper.SqlSyntaxAttribute
member.summary: Indicates whether commands should, when possible, be re-used between operations

member.name: M:Dapper.SqlSyntaxAttribute.#ctor(Dapper.SqlSyntax)
member.summary: Indicates whether commands should, when possible, be re-used between operations

member.name: T:Dapper.StrictTypesAttribute
member.summary: Binds values using strict typing; no type conversions will be applied.

member.name: M:Dapper.StrictTypesAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Binds values using strict typing; no type conversions will be applied.

member.name: P:Dapper.StrictTypesAttribute.Enabled
member.summary: Gets whether strict typing is enabled.

member.name: T:Dapper.TypeAccessor
member.summary: Provides an API to access object properties via indexers.

member.summary: Create a over the provided sequence, optionally specifying the members to include.

member.summary: Create a over the provided sequence, optionally specifying the members to include.

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeAccessor.CreateAccessor``1(``0,Dapper.TypeAccessor{``0})
member.summary: Create a that accesses the specified typed instance.

member.summary: Provides an API to access object properties via indexers.

member.summary: Create a new instance.

member.name: P:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.MemberCount
member.summary: Gets the number of members accessible via this instance.

member.name: P:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets or sets member values by index.

member.name: P:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets or sets member values by name.

member.name: M:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.GetValue``1(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a typed member value by index.

member.name: M:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.SetValue``1(System.Int32,``0)
member.summary: Sets a typed member value by index.

member.name: M:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.GetType(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the type of the specified member.

member.name: M:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.GetName(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets the name of the specified member.

member.name: M:Dapper.ObjectAccessor`1.IsNullable(System.Int32)
member.summary: Indicates whether the specified member can be null.

member.name: T:Dapper.TypeAccessor`1
member.summary: Provides an API to access object properties via indexers.

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeAccessor`1.TryIndex(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Attempt to identify the named member, using either exact or inexact (case-insensitive, ignores underscores) matching.

member.summary: Indicates whether the specified member can be null.

member.summary: Gets the number of members accessible via this instance.

member.summary: Gets the type of the specified member.

member.summary: Gets the name of the specified member.

member.summary: Gets or sets member values by index.

member.summary: Indicates whether the specified value is null.

member.summary: Gets or sets member values by name.

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeAccessor`1.UnsafePun``2(``0)
member.summary: Coerce a value-type from one type to another; this is primarily used to side-step generics, or to switch between enums and their underlying primitive value

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeAccessor`1.NormalizedHash(System.String)
member.summary: Provides a reliable string hashing function that ignores case, whitespace and underscores

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeAccessor`1.NormalizedEquals(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Compares a string for equality against a pre-normalized comparand, ignoring case, whitespace and underscores

member.name: T:Dapper.TypeHandlerAttribute`2
member.summary: Specify that should be used as a custom type-handler when processing values of type

member.name: T:Dapper.TypeHandler`1
member.summary: Process a parameter value of type

member.summary: Configure and assign a value for a parameter

member.name: M:Dapper.TypeHandler`1.Parse(System.Data.Common.DbParameter)
member.summary: Interpret the output value from a parameter

member.name: T:Dapper.UnifiedCommand
member.summary: Represents the state associated with a or (where supported).
member.remarks: Only the current command is available to the caller.

member.name: P:Dapper.UnifiedCommand.Command
member.summary: The associated with the current operation; this may be null for batch commands.

member.summary: Specifies whether to use [System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations.Schema.ColumnAttribute] for additional behavioral configuration

member.name: M:Dapper.UseColumnAttributeAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.param: enable usage of [Column] attribute

member.summary: Encapsulates a inside another, allowing for command post-processing etc

member.summary: Gets the underlying behind this instance

member.summary: Gets the underlying behind this instance
